title: Golden Flaky Ukrainian Pastry Pilaf
description: A smoky and flaky Ukrainian dish featuring ghee, this crowd-pleaser is perfect for any occasion, and its ingredients are friendly to Buddhist temple dietary preferences. It is a delicious and savory pilaf that combines the richness of ghee with the heartiness of grains and vegetables.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the ghee over medium heat and saute the onion and carrot until they are tender.
Add the mushroom to the skillet and cook until it is tender and lightly browned.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked rice, sauteed vegetables, and a pinch of salt and pepper, and mix well.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour and egg to make the pastry dough.
Roll out the pastry dough to a large rectangle and spread the rice and vegetable mixture over the center of the dough.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a large square or rectangle, and press the edges together to seal the pastry.
Place the pastry on a large baking sheet and brush the top with a little bit of ghee.
Bake the pastry in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until it is golden brown.
Remove the pastry from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
